message: Getting 500 errors on /api/v2/users endpoint. Started after the nginx config update this morning. Anyone else seeing this?
Yes! I see it too. Looks like DB connection pool is exhausted. Seeing 'QueuePool limit reached' in auth-service logs.
Also getting CORS error on /api/v2/auth after the nginx change. The Access-Control-Allow-Origin header is missing.
CORS issue is related to the nginx reload. I'll run nginx -s reload and verify headers. Give me 5 minutes.
Done. CORS headers are back on /api/v2/auth. The reload fixed it. Checking the connection pool now.
Payment gateway is still blocked. Stripe sandbox still unreachable. SSL cert renewal from the vendor is taking too long - it has been 8 days.
The dashboard feature is at risk. If the /api/v2/users 500 error is not fixed today, we cannot complete integration tests before sprint deadline.
I increased the DB pool size to 50 in db.yaml and restarted auth-service. Connection pool exhaustion should be resolved now.
Confirmed. /api/v2/users is returning 200 now. Pool fix worked. Still no ticket created for the CORS issue that happened this morning.
Sprint deadline is June 5. Dashboard and payment are both at risk. Should we flag this in the standup?
